page: Amazon Web Services r6i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 128 virtual CPUs powered by 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 1024 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core and memory capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier status in PassMark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests, placing in the top 10% of tested hardware. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests rank in the top 25%, supported by strong memory bandwidth results, including 334.71 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall within the average range, indicating that workloads must leverage parallel execution to maximize the hardware's capabilities.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated resource allocation, the r6i.32xlarge offers high resource density for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing for flexible network storage provisioning. This profile is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, high-throughput web serving, data-intensive software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks that benefit from massive RAM capacity and high memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs from 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8375C processor and 1024 GB of DDR4 RAM. Built on the AWS Nitro System, this x86_64 instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in PassMark CPU, encryption, and extended instruction tests, alongside strong multi-core execution and memory bandwidth. Single-core performance remains average, making parallelization essential. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and complex software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and encryption performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual server featuring 1024 GB of RAM and 128 vCPUs for demanding in-memory database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors
Top-tier PassMark CPU and encryption benchmark performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
